title: NY should expand Kendra’s Law (Guest Opinion by Dr. Barry Perlman)
summary: Opinion piece by retired psychiatrist Dr. Barry Perlman advocates for expanding Kendra's Law (Assisted Outpatient Treatment) in New York, citing 2005 OMH research showing reductions in harmful behaviors, homelessness, and incarceration. Perlman critiques civil liberties organizations' opposition to AOT expansion, arguing their arguments do not address the significant cohort of seriously mentally ill patients who cannot engage in voluntary treatment. The piece reflects ongoing debate over involuntary mental health intervention policy at the state level.

political_signal.description: Dr. Perlman champions expansion of Kendra's Law/AOT in Gov. Hochul's 2025 budget, positioning it as evidence-based policy to address serious mental illness. Aligns with NAMI-NYS endorsement of bills S3474/A5538.
